messages.text: Opening a thread on TeraCorp. They pay $48k/year and are asking for a two-month refund after the Nov 3 outage.
Tagging @jordan-kim for approval since this is already heated.
Wrong person. I meant @jordan-mee. Pulling Priya in too because this is crossing from support into retention.
I am in. From the incident channel I have customer-visible impact at about 3h47m, though the postmortem will probably round it to four hours.
Customer wording was explicit: 'We expect two full months refunded or credited.' They tied it to lost launch time and trust.
Two months is too rich. Policy gives us room for 30 days at the high end, not 60. We should not anchor the customer there.
Agree we should not promise anything in Slack. Marcus, tell them we are reviewing options after the four-hour outage.
Understood. I will hold the line and move this to email with Jordan and Femi.
